meta_description: Here you will find the Xometry Manufacturing Standards. This document contains standards for our CNC Manufacturing, Additive Manufacturing (3D Printing), Sheet Metal, Urethane Casting, and Injection Molding processes.

sections.heading: Our Manufacturing Standards
sections.text: Our Manufacturing Standards Xometry has developed a comprehensive set of Design Guides to assist you in optimizing your design for the manufacturing method selected; please consult them for critical tips and tricks.

sections.heading: Workmanship Standards
sections.text: Workmanship Standards Orders with Xometry will meet the minimum workmanship standards outlined below (as applicable to the process). If your project requires a level of workmanship that goes beyond the standards listed below, please clearly list the requirement in your engineering drawings, purchase orders, or specifications.Â Finished Surfaces Cosmetics Paint coverage on surfaces will be uniform, including adjacent materials of assemblies. Finished surfaces will be free of defects, including chips, scrapes, or other damage. Parts made with Sheet Cutting and Sheet Metal Fabrication have different cosmetics standards. Please see their sections below for details. Mill Steps, Tooling, and Chat...

sections.heading: CNC Machining and Turning
sections.text: CNC Machining and Turning For features of size (Length, width, height, diameter) and location (position, concentricity, symmetry) +/- .005â (metals) or +/- .010 (plastics and composites) following ISO 2768 unless otherwise specified. As machined surface finish is 125 Ra or better. Machine tool marks may leave a swirl-like pattern. Sharp edges will be broken and deburred by default. Critical edges that must be left sharp should be noted and specified on a print. Clear or transparent plastics will be matte or have translucent swirl marks on any machined face. Bead blasting will leave a frosted finish on clear plastics. Xometry cannot guarantee tolerances on foam or similar compressible mate...

sections.text: CNC Orientation and Form General Tolerances for Metals Table: Parallelism, Perpendicularity, Cylindricity, Circularity, Concentricity, Flatness, and Straightness over Part Length Part Length Orientation and Form Tolerance (inch) Angularity Tolerance (degree) Part Length 0 to 12" Orientation and Form Tolerance (inch) .005" Angularity Tolerance (degree) 0.5Â° Part Length 12" to 24" Orientation and Form Tolerance (inch) .010" Angularity Tolerance (degree) 0.5Â° Part Length 24" - 36" Orientation and Form Tolerance (inch) 1/64" (.016") Angularity Tolerance (degree) 1Â° Part Length 36" - 60" Orientation and Form Tolerance (inch) 1/32" (.031") Angularity Tolerance (degree) 1Â° Part Length Over 60"...

sections.heading: Die Casting
sections.text: Die Casting Typical mold machined tolerances are +/- .010" when machining the mold and an additional +/- .001" per in. when calculating shrink rate. Tighter as-molded tolerances can be requested and may increase the cost of tooling. Additionally, many tight tolerances require the mold to be manufactured, sampled, and groomed. Xometry will mill to a steel-safe condition on critical features. Critical toleranced features and surface finishes may require post-machining and should be noted in the quote prior to order. Post-machined features will follow Xometryâs CNC manufacturing standards. Xometry will produce die cast components according to the net-shape (as-cast) model provided by the cus...

sections.text: Learn More About Die Casting Custom Die Casting Services Custom aluminum and zinc die casting for complex metal parts | Cost-effective tooling and rapid, scalable production | ISO 9001:2015, ISO 13485, IATF 16949:2016, AS9100D certified. ITAR registered. Start A Die Casting Quote Custom Die Casting Services

sections.heading: Plastic Injection Molding
sections.text: Plastic Injection Molding Typical mold machined tolerances are +/- .005" when machining the mold and an additional +/- .002" per in. when calculating for shrink rate. Tighter tolerances can be requested and may increase the cost of tooling. Additionally, many tight tolerances require the mold to be manufactured, sampled, and groomed. Xometry will mill to a steel-safe condition on critical features. Part-to-part repeatability is typically under +/- .004". Lead time stated is for first article shipment. Remaining production time is confirmed after first article approval. Typical first article shipments are 10 pieces but may vary based on size, origin, and material. Xometry cannot guarantee a...

sections.heading: Metal Extrusion
sections.text: New! Metal Extrusion All quotes are based upon the assumption that designs have adequate wall thicknesses, radii, and feature sets designed for manufacturability in this process. Critical-to-function tolerances should be discussed at the time of quoting to ensure that tooling strategy, check gages (if applicable), and other quality controls are encompassed in the quote. Linear cross-section dimensions are +/- .010", or +/- .006â per inch, whichever is greater, excluding wall thickness. Wall thickness is +/- 10% of the specified dimension. Cross-section angularity for external corners is +/- 1 degree, and +/- 2 degrees for internal angles. Critical toleranced features and surface finishes...

sections.text: Plastic Extrusion All quotes are based upon the assumption that designs have adequate wall thicknesses, radii, and feature sets designed for manufacturability in this process. Critical-to-function tolerances should be discussed at the time of quoting to ensure that tooling strategy, check gages (if applicable), and other quality controls are encompassed in the quote. Critical toleranced features and surface finishes may require post-machining and should be noted in the quote before ordering. Post-machined features will follow Xometryâs CNC manufacturing standards for plastics. Customer-provided models must contain appropriate material for additional post-processing efforts, such as post-m...

sections.heading: Sheet Cutting Service
sections.text: Sheet Cutting Service Thickness tolerances are independent of cutting tolerances, as they rely on the raw stock materialâs tolerance range. Edge to edge tolerances is +/- .010â nominal on the top face of the plate/sheet. Thicker materials may have a tolerance deviation on the bottom face due to tapers inherent in laser cutting, waterjet cutting. Xometry cannot guarantee flatness call outs for sheet cut materials except on ground stock or tooling plate (e. g. MIC6). Holes of .100â or smaller in diameter may be slightly larger on the top face than standard tolerances due to the cutter piercing the material near the hole's cut line. A small bump or divot in the material or a different ed...

sections.heading: Sheet Metal Fabrication
sections.text: Sheet Metal Fabrication See Sheet Cutting standards for edge-to-edge and other cutting tolerances over flat surfaces. Sharp edges will be broken and deburred by default. Critical edges that must be left sharp should be noted and specified on a drawing. Specific sheet metal designs require custom tooling and will be flagged for a manual quote: hems, curled flanges and rolled sheets, stamped parts, and welded assemblies. If your part includes these features, please allow our manual quote team to review and provide you with an accurate cost and lead time.

ITAR registered. Start Your New Instant Quote Sheet Metal Fabrication Services

sections.heading: Metal Stamping
sections.text: Metal Stamping All quotes are based upon the assumption that designs have an adequate draft, radii, relief cuts, and feature sets for manufacturability in this process. Critical-to-function tolerances should be discussed at the time of quoting to ensure that tooling strategy, check gages (if applicable), and other quality controls are encompassed in the quote. Raw material such as coiled strip is not flat by nature and can show through on flat stamped or coined components. Dimensions apply to the inside of the formed feature. Angular tolerances of +/- 1 degree are typical. Stamped parts will be free of splits and other forming defects. Punch and die-cut features, such as edges, will have a...

sections.heading: Learn More About Stamping
sections.text: Learn More About Stamping Metal Stamping Services High Quality Metal Stamped Parts From Short Runs To Production In The Millions. Get Your Stamping Quote Metal Stamping Services

sections.heading: Laser Tube Cutting
sections.text: Laser Tube Cutting Cut edges will be normal-to-surface as a default. Normal-to-edge cuts will require agreement at the time of order. Thickness tolerances are independent of cutting tolerances, as they rely on the raw stock materialâs tolerance range. Edge-to-edge tolerances of laser cut features are +/- .010â nominal on the inner face of the tube. Holes of .100â or smaller in diameter may be slightly larger than standard tolerances due to material pierce happening near the holeâs profile. Holes that require drilling or machining precision require a manual review during quoting. A small bump of material or a different edge condition may be present at the lead-in and lead-out in a cu...

sections.heading: Learn More About Laser Tube Cutting
sections.text: Learn More About Laser Tube Cutting Laser Tube Cutting Services High-quality tube laser cutting for metal rectangular, square, and round tubes | Free standard shipping on US and international orders | International prototype pricing includes tariffs Get an Instant Laser Tube Cutting Quote Laser Tube Cutting Services

sections.heading: Tube Bending
sections.text: Tube Bending Cut edges will be normal-to-surface as a default. Normal-to-edge cuts will require agreement at the time of order. Tube bending manufacturing standards reflect rotary drawn with or without mandrel bending techniques depending on the project's needs. Compression bending, roll bending, freeform, or other bending methods will require functional specifications agreed upon at the time of order. Tube thickness tolerances are independent of cutting tolerances, as they rely on the tolerance range of the raw stock material. The Center line radius (CLR) will be matched to the closest common tooling, not to exceed +/- .25" of the design. Wall thickness deviation at bends is normal, as it...

sections.heading: Learn More About Tube Bending
sections.text: Learn More About Tube Bending Tube Bending Services Xometry offers precision tube bending services for round metal tube stock to meet your needs. Get an Instant Tube Bending Quote Tube Bending Services

sections.heading: Urethane Casting
sections.text: Urethane Casting Tolearance are +/- .010â or +/- .003â per inch, whichever is larger, is typical. Irregular or overly-thick geometries may cause deviances or deflection due to shrinkage. A shrinkage rate of +0.15% can be expected due to thermal expansion of the liquid, and the response of the flexible mold. Surface finish is externally smoothed to a satin or matte surface. Grow lines may be present on internal or difficult-to-access features. Polishing or custom finishes must be clearly defined and agreed upon at the point of order. Sharp corners and text may appear slightly rounded.

sections.heading: Learn More About Urethane Casting

sections.heading: Metal Binder Jetting 3D Printing
sections.text: Metal Binder Jetting 3D Printing Depending on the partâs size and geometry, parts may shrink 0.8% - 2.5% after processing. Internal geometries, such as slots and holes, may shrink as much as 5%. Xometry does not guarantee tolerances on the first order of a new design. Improved tolerances may be possible with a manual quote review, after successful completion of a prototype build, and must be approved on a case-by-case basis. Metal binder jet machines print in layers of .004â (0.10 mm), and parts are typically oriented in the lowest Z direction. For successful processing, parts with features below .030â (0.75 mm) will need to be thickened. The minimum feature size should be above .040"...

sections.heading: Learn More about Metal Binder Jetting

sections.heading: Carbon DLSâ¢ 3D Printing
sections.text: Carbon DLSâ¢ 3D Printing +/- .003â for the first inch is typical, plus +/- .010â for every inch thereafter. However, Xometry does not guarantee tolerances on the first order of a new design. Tolerance expectations can vary across materials (e.g., elastomeric versus rigid). See the table below, General Accuracy for DLS Prints, for material-specific information. Stresses during build, support strategy, and other geometry considerations may cause deviation in tolerances and flatness. Parts with thicker geometries, flat or broad parts, and parts with uneven wall thicknesses may be prone to significant deviations or warp. Modeled threads or precision features may have limited functionality...

sections.text: Learn More about Carbon DLSâ¢ Carbon Digital Light Synthesisâ¢ (DLSâ¢) 3D Printing Service High-Performing Prototypes and Serialized Production Parts | Free Shipping on All US Orders Start Your New Instant Quote Carbon Digital Light Synthesisâ¢ (DLSâ¢) 3D Printing Service

sections.heading: DMLS 3D Printing
sections.text: DMLS 3D Printing +/- .005â for the first inch is typical, plus +/- .002â for every inch thereafter. However, Xometry does not guarantee tolerances on the first order of a new design. Tolerance expectations can vary across different materials (e.g., stainless steel versus aluminum). Internal stresses during build, support strategy, and other geometry considerations may cause deviation in tolerances and flatness. Items and geometries which require strict flatness are not a good fit for this process. Modeled threads or precision features may have limited functionality as printed. Tapping or the addition of threaded inserts is recommended for best function. General tolerances apply before s...

sections.heading: FDM 3D Printing
sections.text: FDM 3D Printing +/- a single build layer thickness for the first inch and +/- .002â for every inch thereafter. .010â layer thickness is used for parts fitting within a 14â X 16â footprint (Fortus 450) .013â layer thickness is used for parts exceeding 14â X 16â footprint (Fortus 900) .008" layer thickness used for Desktop FDM/FFF platforms (e.g., Prusa MK3S/MK4 or Bambu Lab X1C) General Purpose FDM materials (e.g., General Purpose ABS) may vary in color and layer height based on supply and equipment used. Branded materials (e.g., Stratasys) are recommended if strict consistency is required between orders and batches. Guaranteed tolerances may be possible with a manual quote rev...

sections.heading: HP MJF 3D Printing
sections.text: HP MJF 3D Printing Parts with thicker geometries, flat or broad parts (>7â), and parts with uneven wall thicknesses will be prone to significant deviations or warp due to variable thermal shrinkage and stress. Modeled threads or precision features may have limited functionality as printed. Tapping or the addition of threaded inserts is recommended for best function. The color of naturally grey materials may be inconsistent depending on feature size and build orientation. Dyed black is recommended for production parts. Multi-color (CB) parts may appear less vibrant and slightly more muted than they do in the digital 3D model. Xometry cannot guarantee exact color matching. Vapor smoothing c...

sections.heading: HP MJF Prototyping Tolerances
sections.text: HP MJF Prototyping Tolerances In the table below, you will find our standard MJF tolerances for prototype orders . These include one-off or first-time prints, non-production orders, and auto-quoted orders that have not received a manual engineering review.

sections.heading: HP MJF Engineer Reviewed & Production Tolerances
sections.text: HP MJF Engineer Reviewed & Production Tolerances Tolerances tighter than prototype tolerancing can be guaranteed after additional engineering review . This includes higher volume production part orders and orders manually optimized by our engineering teams, often after an initial prototyping run.

sections.heading: Polyjet 3D Printing
sections.text: Polyjet 3D Printing +/- .004â for the first inch is typical, plus +/- .002â for every inch thereafter. Positive features below .040" in size may not fully resolve, be extremely fragile, or be damaged during post-processing. A feature size of .050" or greater is recommended. Rubber-like materials represent an approximation of shore A values and may vary between geometries. General tolerances apply before secondary finishing or post-processing unless otherwise specified. Surface roughness ~32-80 Î¼in Ra.
